records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Indicted (n*dīt"d); p. pr. & vb. n. Indicting.]

defs: To write; to compose; to dictate; to indite.
To appoint publicly or by authority; to proclaim or announce.
quotes: I am told shall have no Lent indicted this year.

defs: To charge with a crime, in due form of law, by the finding or presentment of a grand jury; to bring an indictment against; as, to indict a man for arson. It is the peculiar province of a grand jury to indict, as it is of a house of representatives to impeach.
